scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: 8 read streams (the wave2 legacy-parity projects stream plus people, companies, tags, time_entries as global streams, tasklists/milestones fanned out per-project via the engine's fan_out dialect, and tasks as a global cross-project stream) and 9 write actions covering project/tasklist/task/milestone/company/time-entry create-update-complete mutations. Excluded: the ~150-endpoint object catalog beyond these (boards/kanban, calendar events, comments, files, notebooks, links, invoices/billing, messages/posts, webhooks, workload, search, custom fields, risks, ratings/roles) as out-of-scope breadth-vs-cost triage; every V1 write body requires a resource-name wrapper key (e.g. POST /projects.json expects {"project": {...}}), modeled here by declaring the wrapper key itself as a required nested-object record_schema field (the engine's body_type:json construction sends record fields verbatim, so a record whose one top-level key IS the wrapper name produces the exact wrapped body the legacy V1 API requires - no engine change needed).

excluded.reason: single-project detail; identical record shape to the projects list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
irreversibly deletes a project and every tasklist/task/milestone/time-entry nested under it
